intent.what: Declarative conformance profile: dark-agents MCPs (dark-research-mcp, dark-memory-mcp, future) conform to MCP 2025-06-18 with extensions. The dark-recall plugin becomes optional opencode-specific glue, not 'the integration layer'. Coexistence versioning (cx.v1/cx.v2/cx.v3) is declared in serverInfo.coexistence_group. Shared state via dark.db with table-level ownership.
intent.why: OSINT confirms MCP itself IS the bridge. The harness (Claude, opencode, Cursor, VS Code) is MCP-native per the [MCP architecture](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/architecture). Writing per-MCP integration glue reinvents what MCP already provides. This document stops the diversification of connection patterns.
five_commitments: No per-MCP integration glue at the harness layer. Harness is MCP-native.
dark-recall plugin is opencode-specific optional glue, not 'the integration layer'.
Coexistence contract declared in serverInfo.coexistence_group, not custom headers.
Every dark-agents MCP conforms to MCP 2025-06-18 with the profile in section 2.
dark-research-mcp dark_mem_* stays for legacy; new features in dark_memory_*.
intent.supersedes: previous per-MCP integration design (see sub-spec 7 dark-recall v2.3 and sub-spec 8 deprecation shim — these are now scoped to their actual role: opencode-specific UX + legacy compat)

must_have: BRIDGE_AND_COEXISTENCE.md published at vibe-flow/main/ and referenced by DARK_MEMORY_MCP_RFC.md
dark-memory-mcp initialize response includes coexistence_group=dark-agents/memory and coexistence_version=cx.v2
tools/list returns exactly 25 dark_memory_* orchestrators in canonical order
dark-recall plugin v2.3 detects both servers and prefers dark_memory_* with dark_mem_* fallback
Failure isolation: killing one server does not block the other
tests/conformance/inspector_test.go runs the MCP Inspector against both servers and asserts the conformance profile
